ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the customer */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "signup_date" TEXT /* Date when the customer signed up */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the customer */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the customer */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the customer is currently active */,
  "last_purchase_date" TEXT /* Date of the last purchase */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "transactions" (
  "transaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each transaction */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer making the transaction */,
  "transaction_date" TEXT /* Date when the transaction occurred */,
  "amount" REAL /* Total amount of the transaction */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Payment method used (e.g., credit card, cash) */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product purchased */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of items purchased */,
  "store_id" INTEGER /* ID of the store where the transaction occurred */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the transaction (e.g., online, in-store) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("transaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_transactions_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each segment */,
  "segment_name" TEXT /* Name of the customer segment (e.g., High Value, At Risk) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the criteria for the segment */,
  "segment_criteria" TEXT /* Detailed criteria for the segment */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date when the segment was created */,
  "last_update_date" TEXT /* Date when the segment was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rfm_scores" (
  "rfm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each RFM score entry */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer associated with the score */,
  "recency" INTEGER /* Recency score indicating how recent the last purchase was */,
  "frequency" INTEGER /* Frequency score indicating the number of purchases */,
  "monetary" REAL /* Monetary score representing total spending */,
  "classification" INTEGER /* Overall classification based on RFM analysis */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the RFM scores were last updated */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the corresponding customer segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rfm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rfm_scores_segment_id FOREIGN KEY ("segment_id") REFERENCES customer_segments ("segment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rfm_scores_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marketing_campaigns" (
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each marketing campaign */,
  "campaign_name" TEXT /* Name of the marketing campaign */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the campaign */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the campaign */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget allocated for the campaign */,
  "target_segment" INTEGER /* ID of the segment targeted by the campaign */,
  "campaign_type" TEXT /* Type of campaign (e.g., email, social media, SMS) */,
  "campaign_status" TEXT /* Status of the campaign (e.g., active, completed, paused) */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the campaign */,
  "last_modified_by" TEXT /* User who last modified the campaign */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date when the campaign was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("campaign_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_marketing_campaigns_target_segment FOREIGN KEY ("target_segment") REFERENCES customer_segments ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "campaign_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each campaign result entry */,
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* ID of the related marketing campaign */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who responded to the campaign */,
  "response" TEXT /* Response type (e.g., clicked, purchased) */,
  "response_date" TEXT /* Date of customer response */,
  "conversion_rate" REAL /* Conversion rate of the campaign */,
  "revenue_generated" REAL /* Revenue generated from the campaign */,
  "cost_per_acquisition" REAL /* Cost of acquiring a new customer through the campaign */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_campaign_results_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_campaign_results_campaign_id FOREIGN KEY ("campaign_id") REFERENCES marketing_campaigns ("campaign_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback entry */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer providing the feedback */,
  "feedback_date" TEXT /* Date when the feedback was submitted */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Star rating given by the customer (1-5) */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments provided by the customer */,
  "channel" TEXT /* Channel through which the feedback was provided (e.g., email, phone, in-person) */,
  "feedback_source" TEXT /* Source of the feedback (e.g., survey, customer service interaction) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_feedback_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_interactions" (
  "interaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interaction entry */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer involved in the interaction */,
  "interaction_date" TEXT /* Date of the interaction */,
  "interaction_type" TEXT /* Type of interaction (e.g., email, phone call, in-person) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes regarding the interaction */,
  "interaction_channel" TEXT /* Channel of interaction (e.g., email, phone, in-person) */,
  "duration" TEXT /* Duration of the interaction */,
  "interaction_status" TEXT /* Status of the interaction (e.g., resolved, pending) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_interactions_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "loyalty_programs" (
  "program_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each loyalty program */,
  "program_name" TEXT /* Name of the loyalty program */,
  "points_required" INTEGER /* Points required to join the program */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the loyalty program */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date when the loyalty program was created */,
  "last_update_date" TEXT /* Date when the loyalty program was last updated */,
  "benefits" TEXT /* Detailed description of the benefits offered by the program */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("program_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "loyalty_points" (
  "points_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the points entry */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer earning the points */,
  "program_id" INTEGER /* ID of the loyalty program associated with the points */,
  "points_earned" INTEGER /* Total points earned by the customer */,
  "points_redeemed" INTEGER /* Total points redeemed by the customer */,
  "points_expiration_date" TEXT /* Date when the points will expire */,
  "points_earned_date" TEXT /* Date when the points were earned */,
  "points_redeemed_date" TEXT /* Date when the points were redeemed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("points_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_loyalty_points_program_id FOREIGN KEY ("program_id") REFERENCES loyalty_programs ("program_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_loyalty_points_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_accounts" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for account access */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, marketing, analyst) */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  "account_status" TEXT /* Status of the user account (e.g., active, locked) */,
  "last_password_change_date" TEXT /* Date when the password was last changed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_accounts_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions granted to this role */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the role */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the role was created */,
  "last_modified_by" TEXT /* User who last modified the role */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date when the role was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "crm_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user performing the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action taken */,
  "action_date" TEXT /* Date when the action was performed */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of action (e.g., create, update, delete) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the action was performed */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent (e.g., browser, device) from which the action was performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crm_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES user_accounts ("user_id")
)
